- **No P0/P1/P2.** One P3 (non-blocking): `command-evidence.json` stores exit codes (all 0) but empty stdout/stderr for every command, so command-level evidence is exit-code-only and reported (not re-executable by a read-only reviewer). Mitigated by the generated contract JSON embedding `validation.valid=true` with empty error arrays, which I verified directly.
- The descriptor-only `migration_platform_domain_implementation_descriptor_verified` claim is supported: deliverable/micro-title distributions match the baseline exactly, validators enforce runtime-closed/no-payload/no-cross-tenant boundaries, tests assert the right invariants, no-write attestation is comprehensive, and the authority boundary keeps Claude read-only/non-final with human approval gating any runtime opening (Hermes used only as deterministic control-plane).

DIRECTLY VERIFIED via Read/Grep/Glob: (1) registry.js CP757 requirements match the pack baseline exactly — deliverable distribution {implementation:23,ui:5,fixture:2,test:6,hermes_evidence:2,claude_review:2}=40 and micro_title counts {Type And Shape Definition:12,Primary Implementation Slice:22,Secondary Workflow Slice:6}; (2) required_section_rows slices map correctly to included units (M02 S09-S20, M03 S01-S22, M04 S01-S06); (3) validators.js enforces descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, no sensitive/source payloads, no source-system/import runtime opened, no cross-tenant access, no permission-decision or audit-body leakage, plus gate/handoff/authority-boundary checks; (4) model.test.js asserts CP756/CP757 invariants incl. runtime-closed, read-only Claude, correct handoff, and latest contract projection; (5) the generated contracts/migration-platform-contract.json embeds validation.valid=true with empty errors arrays for both packs' coverage and descriptor blocks and matches every test assertion; (6) MIGRATION_PLATFORM_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is comprehensive (no product-state writes, no db rows, no object storage, no external/source-system runtime); (7) import-plan.js/mappers.js keep runtime closed, dry-run/human-approval required, no cross-tenant, no payloads; (8) authority boundary holds — claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, Hermes packets emit_runtime_receipt=false/writes_product_state=false (deterministic control-plane pattern, not product runtime); (9) manifest pre-review state correct (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, exactly_one_valid_pack_review_required=true, valid_review_receipts=[]); (10) plan live_state and next-pack-queue put CP00-757 as live latest with CP00-758 queued at RP25.P01.M04.S07; (11) all referenced npm scripts exist and root `npm test` includes packages/migration/test; (12) no stale review receipt/raw-output exists (correctly generated post-review); unrelated dirty/untracked files (incl. the LDIP invalid-attempt file and workbook docs) are correctly classified out-of-scope. REPORTED BUT NOT RE-EXECUTED (read-only, no Bash): the command-evidence.json exit codes (all 0); these were not independently re-run. The pack does not claim runtime_ready or enterprise trust, so the human/enterprise-trust authority boundary is outside this receipt. One non-blocking P3 on command-evidence output auditability; no P0/P1/P2.","findings":[{"id":"CP757-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"command-evidence.json records exit codes but empty stdout/stderr for all commands","file":"docs/closeout-packs/cp00-757/command-evidence.json","line":27,"details":"Every command entry in command-evidence.json (including npm test, npm run validate, npm run weighted:validate, node --test of the migration model tests, etc.) records exit_code:0 but stores stdout:"" and stderr:"". A grep for any non-empty stdout returned zero matches. This means the evidence file is exit-code-only: it does not preserve test pass counts, validation summary lines, or other output that would let a reviewer confirm WHAT each command reported. As a read-only reviewer with no Bash, I could not re-execute these commands, so the command-level pass evidence is reported-only. This is partially mitigated because the actual computed validation results are independently embedded in the generated contracts/migration-platform-contract.json (validation.valid=true with empty errors arrays for both packs' coverage and descriptor blocks), which I verified directly, and because all exit codes are 0. It is a weakness in auditability rather than a correctness defect.","recommendation":"In future command-evidence captures, persist at least summary stdout (e.g., node:test pass/fail tallies and validator 'passed' lines) so command outcomes are auditable from the evidence file itself rather than only via exit codes. No change required to close CP00-757; substantive validation is independently verifiable in the embedded contract projection.","post_review_disposition":"accepted_non_blocking; evidence-quality observation only; mitigated by embedded contract validation results and exit_code=0; does not block pack or goal closeout"}],"finding_counts":{"p0":0,"p1":0,"p2":0,"p3":1,"reported_p0":0,"reported_p1":0,"reported_p2":0,"reported_p3":1}},"terminal_reason":"completed","fast_mode_state":"off","uuid":"a330ee03-c5d0-4f3a-b3af-3daa079b1a8b"}
